title: Botox Tutorial For Men | Botox Injection Patterns | Forehead, Corrugators & Crows Feet
filename: Botox Tutorial For Men ｜ Botox Injection Patterns ｜ Forehead, Corrugators & Crows Feet.en.vtt
excerpt: This week I'd like to share with you a simple treatment on a male men are different to inject than females obviously and it's all about the eyebrows so I hope you can learn a lot from this very simple basic treatment that we all need to know when you start treating men so this is…

segments.text: this week I'd like to share with you a simple treatment on a male men are different to inject than females obviously and it's all about the eyebrows so I hope you can learn a lot from this very simple basic treatment that we all need to know when you start treating men so this is a seven-point injection strategy designed to minimize movement it is acceptable to use a fivepoint or even a three-point strategy if you don't mind a little bit of lateral movement the downside of this technique is a lateral pull which some patients do not like discuss it with your patient and choose the best plan for them remember the lateral injections are most likely to affect the frontalis and cause eyebrow heaviness so you must be precise here treating the frontalis is the trickiest muscle note in masculine faces the frontalis muscle often goes higher up and is stronger more superiorly into the hairline

this becomes a problem if you treat with a feminine pattern you will get additional movement high up near the hairline and follow-ups will occur from a safety perspective the first thing to do is Mark out the areas where you do not want to inject here I'm marking out the inferior margin of where I will inject leaving a 2 cm margin approximately from the eyebrows it's also help helpful to consider where the lines and wrinkles are here I'm also marking out where I see the appon Neurosis where there's minimal movement and this is simply to save on bot toxin if you were to inject in this area it would not cause a side effect it's just a waste of money what you're left with then is a smaller area to focus your attention on to decide where to inject we now need to consider how far does Bim toxin diffuse from an injection point the rule of thumb is about 1.5 CM this enables me to space out my injections it's effectively a marble size area of diffusion and the goal now is to fill this frontales area with marble sized injection points to make it easier to space them out choose

your first medial injection point and then your most lateral injection Point bearing in mind the spread of the toxin it then makes it easier and clearer to decide where to put your injections between those points and that's what it looks like when done more efficiently with practice if in doubt Place one more superiorly as it's a very safe place to treat men who get lines and wrinkles higher up after a procedure will often refer to them as horns and this is why we need to treat well on the first pass this is true even if the muscle is not that strong as it tends to increase in activity after a procedure where it has not been treated though there are always exceptions a masculine frontales tends to be stronger more Superior and laterally and up into the hairline than a female when assessing the orbicular oculi muscle I tend to start by looking for the thickest part of the muscle which tends to follow the trajectory of the eyebrow you can see that the orbicular ocula is also an accessory muscle for

cheek elevation and this is not something that I want to completely neutralize I don't want to completely neutralize the orbicular oculi or his face will look heavy or sad even when he's not smiling getting rid of the wrinkles at the expense of causing another problem typically this is to look drawn for this reason I make a small adjustment here and I will use lower doses lower down injecting the lella complex the goal here is to reduce the risk of boty toxin making its way into the orbit affecting the levator pulp muscle and secondly to avoid treating the frontalis which overlaps with the corrugator the way I avoid this is by being at the right depth compression during injection is one simple way of reducing the risk

you can see my finger here inferior to the eyebrow compressing on the superior aspect of the orbital I'm also angling my needle so that it's pointing away from the orbit in my mind's eye I'm aiming for a deep Point underneath the Mark I've placed on the forehead I'm not penetrating through the mark left by the pencil corrugator is deep medially intermediate at the intermediate injection point and superficial laterally my injections are deep medially in this case four units although I would commonly use six units in a man if I knew they required more the muscle is less deep here as it is traversing laterally it becomes more superficial so I'm now at intermediate depth for the final injection I'm most lateral I'm medial to the insertion point and injecting 1 to two units procerus is the easiest and safest injection we then repeat the process on the other side of the face we aiming for the same depth in the skin staying medial to the mid pillar line May decrease the chance of affecting the

affecting the frontalis which is a vulnerable spot for causing eyebrow asymmetry or eyebrow tosis treating the forehead though technically the hardest area to plan is the simplest area to inject we only have the frontalis muscle nearby we have skin hypodermis fascia muscle that we want to inject loose aity tissue and the bone the Bim toxin injections are effective in all the layers though probably more effective directly into the muscle or underneath the muscle I aim for injecting into the muscle as I do not like pushing on the bone with the needle I'm going to use for subsequent injections and injecting into the dermis is also more painful and less effective I'm estimating the depth of the muscle here with experience we through skin hypodermis and then into the muscle as the injections are technically easy it's a relatively quick process for a man wanting a relatively natural

treatment two units across in all these areas is sufficient in most people although licenses now include much higher doses of four units per injection point now treating the orbicular oculi the key here to avoid side effects and risks is to stay superficial the skin is very thin we only need to go 1 to 2 mm beneath we'll be approximately through the fat and resting just above the orbicular ocul muscle note how superficial it is many new injectors like to inject all the way in and this is why side effects even simple ones like bruising are more common than they need to be it's particularly important to use accuracy in the inferior part where the risk of zygomaticus major being affected is highest
